---
title: "Technical reference"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Technical reference

## Purpose

This section explains the infrastructure behind the BNR `info-hub`.

Use the **How-To Guides** when you need to perform a task. Use this reference section when you need to understand how the repository, Stata workflow, output bundles, Quarto publication layer, naming rules, and deployment pathway fit together.

The Technical Manual is deliberately separate from the BNR Operations Manual:

- the **Operations Manual** explains how BNR operates as a registry and surveillance system
- the **Technical Manual** explains how to maintain the website, scripts, output bundles, PDF route, and deployment workflow

## Reference pages

| Page | Use it to understand |
|---|---|
| [Repository structure](repository-structure.qmd) | What each top-level folder is for |
| [Compute layer: Stata](compute-layer-stata.qmd) | How Stata remains the primary analytics engine |
| [Output contract](output-contract.qmd) | How generated outputs pass from Stata to Quarto |
| [Quarto publication layer](quarto-publication-layer.qmd) | How HTML pages, PDFs, downloads, and catalogues are published |
| [PDF briefing route](pdf-briefing-route.qmd) | How Typst PDFs are generated from PDF-focused QMD files |
| [Naming rules](naming-rules.qmd) | How files, folders, scripts, and outputs should be named |
| [Confidential data boundary](confidential-data-boundary.qmd) | What may enter the repo and what must remain local-only |
| [GitHub Pages deployment](github-pages-deployment.qmd) | How the static site is published |

## Working principle

The infrastructure should remain understandable to a skilled Stata user who is not a specialist web developer.

When a technical choice is possible, prefer the option that is:

- easier to explain
- easier to inspect
- easier to hand over
- less dependent on hidden automation
- less likely to expose confidential data

:::{.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## How to use this reference section

Start with [Output contract](output-contract.qmd) if you are trying to understand how Stata output becomes web content.

Start with [Confidential data boundary](confidential-data-boundary.qmd) if you are unsure whether a file can be committed to Git.

Start with [Repository structure](repository-structure.qmd) if you are unsure where a file should live.
:::
